source: Self-generated by our own solver. Data lives at poker_solver/charts/pushfold_v1.json; this fixture points to that path rather than duplicating the data inline.
license_notes: Internal artifact — MIT-licensed with the rest of the repo. NOT an external reference; serves as a consistency / self-regression check.
data_file_format_notes: The referenced file is a multi-depth chart. Each depth has 'sb_jam' (frequency to jam per hand class for the SB) and 'bb_call_vs_jam' (frequency to call the jam per hand class for the BB). Hands not listed default to frequency 0.0. Classes use 169-name notation (AA, AKs, AKo, etc.).

config.action_menu_notes: Pure jam/fold action set. SB jams or folds; BB calls or folds. No minraise / limp lines.
config.convention_notes: Brown convention for terminal-utility (winner gets pot+c_loser). DCFR (alpha=1.5, beta=0.0, gamma=2.0), 4000 iters per (depth, position). Final exploitability 0.0001 bb/100 across all depths.

SB_jam.expected_strategy_format_notes: Single-action chart: the referenced data file gives 'jam' frequency per hand. Strategy distribution is [fold = 1 - jam_freq, all_in = jam_freq] for each class. Hands not in the dict default to all-fold (jam_freq = 0.0).
SB_jam.notes: All 169 classes covered (omitted classes default to 0.0). Spans 14 stack depths 2 BB through 15 BB.

BB_call_vs_jam.expected_strategy_format_notes: Strategy distribution is [fold = 1 - call_freq, call = call_freq] for each class.

nash_multiplicity_notes: Pushfold equilibrium is highly Nash-pure (very few mixed-strategy cells). This is the IDEAL diff-test region — disagreement implies an engine bug, not Nash multiplicity.
If a future Premium-A blueprint differs from this self-reference at 2 BB through 15 BB by more than L1 = 0.01 per class, that is a regression signal.

use_as.regression_check_for_premium_a: If Premium-A's blueprint at 20 BB extrapolates BACK to the pushfold region (which it should not directly, since Premium-A starts at 20 BB), an interpolation pass from Premium-A to pushfold depths via the Phase 3 interpolation layer should approximately reproduce these numbers.
use_as.do_not_use_as_external_reference: This is internal self-data. Counting it as an 'external GTO reference' would be circular. Phase 7 diff-test reports SHOULD distinguish 'external reference fixtures' (e.g., hu_100bb_user_supplied_chart_*.json) from 'self-reference fixtures' (this one).
